summary: The OpenRPC definition added to workflow-tools.md diverges from the spec on four requirements. R2 (P0): specs_merged is defined as array-of-strings instead of array-of-objects {spec_id, target}. R4 (P0): all three error definitions are missing the required data.description field. R6 (P1): x-merge-strategies uses a completely different structure and strategy names (section-append, 3-way-merge, conflict-marker) instead of the spec-mandated flat keys {3way, overwrite, create}. R7 (P1): x-post-archive-git uses a steps array with 3 entries instead of the spec-mandated sequence array with 9 named operations. Spec has no Test Plan section so the hard reject rule on missing tests does not apply; rejections are purely from schema mismatches.

issues.title: specs_merged is array-of-strings instead of array-of-objects {spec_id, target}
issues.description: Spec R2 (P0) states: 'Response required array includes specs_merged (array of {spec_id, target})'. The implementation defines items: { "type": "string" } with a plain description. The spec's RPC API section defines items as { "type": "object", "required": ["spec_id", "target"], "properties": { "spec_id": { "type": "string" }, "target": { "type": "string" } } }. This schema mismatch will break clients that expect the structured form to extract spec_id and target_path separately.

issues.title: Error definitions missing data.description field
issues.description: Spec R4 (P0) defines each error with a data object: { "code": -32001, "message": "missing main_spec_ref", "data": { "description": "..." } }. The implementation omits the data field on all three errors (-32001, -32002, -32003) and uses different message text (e.g. 'Missing main_spec_ref — change spec has no main_spec_ref field...' instead of 'missing main_spec_ref'). The spec specifies exact message strings and requires the data.description discriminator for tooling.

issues.title: x-merge-strategies uses wrong structure and wrong strategy names
issues.description: Spec R6 (P1) requires x-merge-strategies to be a flat object with keys '3way', 'overwrite', and 'create', each mapping to a descriptive string: { "3way": "When .base.md sibling exists AND target exists AND git available: git merge-file", "overwrite": "When no .base.md or target already exists: write/overwrite directly", "create": "When target does not exist: create new file" }. The implementation instead uses { "description": "...", "strategies": [{"name": "section-append", ...}, {"name": "3-way-merge", ...}, {"name": "conflict-marker", ...}] } — a different structure with different strategy names that do not match the spec's 3way/overwrite/create taxonomy.

issues.title: x-post-archive-git uses steps array (3 entries) instead of sequence array (9 named operations)
issues.description: Spec R7 (P1) requires x-post-archive-git.sequence to list 9 ordered named steps: [load_config, check_auto_commit, find_git_binary, git_status, git_add, git_commit, check_auto_pr, dispatch_pr_agent, gh_pr_create]. The implementation uses a 'steps' key (not 'sequence') with only 3 entries structured as {step: N, action: '...', condition: '...'} objects. The missing entries omit critical steps such as find_git_binary, git_status, dispatch_pr_agent, and gh_pr_create, making the spec non-conformant.

issues.title: status enum includes undocumented 'conflict' value
issues.description: Spec defines status enum as ["ok", "error"]. The implementation adds a third value 'conflict' not present in the spec. Conflict scenarios are handled via error code -32003 per spec R4; a separate status value is redundant and contradicts the enum definition.

issues.title: result wrapper missing name: MergeResult
issues.description: The spec's RPC API section defines the result as { "name": "MergeResult", "schema": { ... } }. The implementation omits the name field, writing result: { "schema": { ... } } directly. This is a minor deviation but inconsistent with the spec.

issues.title: Missing prompt_path and executor properties in result schema
issues.description: The spec's Changes section lists result.properties as: status, prompt_path, executor, specs_merged[], archive_path, audit_log[], git_commit_sha, pr_url, git_warning, next_actions[]. The implementation omits prompt_path and executor from the properties object. These are not in required[], so it is a soft gap, but they should appear in properties for completeness.

checklist.code_matches_spec_requirements: FAIL — R2 (specs_merged type), R4 (missing error data fields), R6 (x-merge-strategies structure), R7 (x-post-archive-git structure) all diverge from spec
checklist.existing_tests_pass: PASS — no logic changes; spec-file-only change does not break existing tests
